name: Lace Cookies/Lemon-Buttercream Filling
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Jo Merrill
Butter (unsalted): Cut into 1/2 inch pieces and bring to room temperature.
Filling:
1. Dissolve cornstarch in lemon juice.
2. Whisk egg yolks, sugar, and 4 tablespoons butter in a heavy saucepan.
3. Add lemon peel; whisk until combined.
4. Whisk over low heat until smooth and thick enough to mound in a spoon (about 5 minutes). Do not boil.
5. Cool mixture to room temperature, whisking occasionally.
6. Add remaining 6 tablespoons butter, one piece at a time, whisking until smooth.
7. Cover tightly and refrigerate until well chilled. This part can be prepared up to 2 days in advance.
Cookies:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C) and position rack in the center.
2. Dab butter onto rimless baking sheets or use rimmed sheets turned over; line with foil, leaving a 2-inch overhang on short ends.
3. In a large bowl, combine sugar, oats, flour, and baking powder.
4. Add melted butter, milk, molasses, and vanilla; stir until just blended.
5. Let batter stand for 15 minutes.
6. Drop batter onto prepared sheets by heaping teaspoonfuls, forming into 1-inch diameter rounds and spacing about 3 inches apart.
7. Bake until cookies bubble and begin to brown around the edges (about 6-7 minutes).
8. Transfer foil with cookies to a work surface; gently flatten with a spatula if needed.
9. Cool completely.
Before serving:
1. Spread 3 teaspoons of filling evenly on the bottom side of one cookie.
2. Top with another cookie, bottom side up.
3. Repeat with remaining cookies and filling.
